title: Mellow Golden Japanese Chicken Skillet
description: A charred, light Japanese dish featuring Worcestershire sauce, inspired by the Kyushu region, made in a cast-iron skillet. This Buddhist-temple-friendly recipe is perfect for a lazy Sunday.
instructions: Heat the vegetable oil in a large cast-iron skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken to the skillet and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
Remove the chicken from the skillet and set aside.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for 1 minute.
In a small bowl, whisk together the Worcestershire sauce, soy sauce, mirin, and sake.
Pour the sauce mixture into the skillet and bring to a boil.
Return the chicken to the skillet and cook until the sauce has thickened and the chicken is cooked through.
Stir in the chopped green onions.
Serve the chicken and sauce over rice or noodles.
